  1052→                {"error": str(e)},
  1053→            )
  1054→            # Tear down the agent so it doesn't leak in active_agents
  1055→            await self._teardown_agent(agent)
  1056→        finally:
  1057→            # Clean up using the snapshotted session_id, not agent.session_id
  1058→            # which may have been mutated by cycle_agent_to_next_step.
  1059→            logger.debug(
  1060→                f"Monitor cleanup for session {my_session_id} "
  1061→                f"(agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id}, "
  1062→                f"event_present={my_session_id in self.session_status_events})"
  1063→            )
  1064→            if my_session_id in self.session_status_events:
  1065→                del self.session_status_events[my_session_id]
  1066→            self._session_last_activity.pop(my_session_id, None)
  1067→
  1068→    async def _cleanup_session(self, agent: AgentIdentity):
  1069→        """Abort and delete an agent's opencode session.
  1070→
  1071→        Called after agent completion or failure to ensure the session
  1072→        does not linger and consume tokens.
  1073→        """
  1074→        logger.info(f"Cleaning up session {agent.session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id}, worktree={agent.worktree})")
  1075→        await self.opencode.cleanup_session(agent.session_id, directory=agent.worktree)
  1076→
  1077→    def _mark_agent_failed(self, agent_id: str):
  1078→        """Mark an agent failed in DB and clear issue/session references."""
  1079→        self.db.conn.execute(
  1080→            """
  1081→            UPDATE agents
  1082→            SET status = 'failed',
  1083→                current_issue = NULL,
  1084→                session_id = NULL
  1085→            WHERE id = ?
  1086→            """,
  1087→            (agent_id,),
  1088→        )
  1089→        self.db.conn.commit()
  1090→
  1091→    def _release_issue(self, issue_id: str):
  1092→        """Release an issue back to the open queue."""
  1093→        self.db.update_issue_status(issue_id, "open")
  1094→        self.db.conn.execute("UPDATE issues SET assignee = NULL WHERE id = ?", (issue_id,))
  1095→        self.db.conn.commit()
  1096→
  1097→    def _delete_agent_row(self, agent_id: str):
  1098→        """Delete agent row for early spawn-orphan cleanup paths."""
  1099→        self.db.conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF")
  1100→        try:
  1101→            self.db.conn.execute("DELETE FROM agents WHERE id = ?", (agent_id,))
  1102→        finally:
  1103→            self.db.conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
  1104→        self.db.conn.commit()
  1105→
  1106→    async def _best_effort_cleanup(self, label: str, op: Awaitable[Any]):
  1107→        """Run async cleanup operation and suppress failures with debug logging."""
  1108→        try:
  1109→            await op
  1110→        except Exception as e:
  1111→            logger.debug(f"Best-effort cleanup failed ({label}): {e}")
  1112→
  1113→    async def _teardown_agent(self, agent: AgentIdentity, *, remove_worktree: bool = False):
  1114→        """Best-effort cleanup for session, in-memory registration, and worktree."""
  1115→        await self._best_effort_cleanup("cleanup_session", self._cleanup_session(agent))
  1116→
  1117→        if agent.agent_id in self.active_agents:
  1118→            self._unregister_agent(agent.agent_id)
  1119→
  1120→        if remove_worktree and agent.worktree:
  1121→            await self._best_effort_cleanup("remove_worktree", remove_worktree_async(agent.worktree))
  1122→
  1123→    @contextmanager
  1124→    def _agent_handling_scope(self, agent: AgentIdentity, *, handler_name: str):
  1125→        """Guard against double-handling and ensure membership cleanup."""
  1126→        if agent.agent_id not in self.active_agents:
  1127→            logger.debug(f"Skipping {handler_name} for {agent.name} — already removed from active agents")
  1128→            yield False
  1129→            return
  1130→
  1131→        if agent.agent_id in self._handling_agents:
  1132→            logger.debug(f"Skipping {handler_name} for {agent.name} — already being handled")
  1133→            yield False
  1134→            return
  1135→
  1136→        self._handling_agents.add(agent.agent_id)
  1137→        try:
  1138→            yield True
  1139→        finally:
  1140→            self._handling_agents.discard(agent.agent_id)
  1141→
  1142→    async def _dispatch_worker_to_issue(
  1143→        self,
  1144→        *,
  1145→        agent: AgentIdentity,
  1146→        issue: Dict[str, Any],
  1147→        model: str,
  1148→        started_event_type: str,
  1149→        started_event_detail: Dict[str, Any],
  1150→        completed_steps: Optional[List[str]] = None,
  1151→    ):
  1152→        """Shared prompt + dispatch flow for spawn and epic cycling."""
  1153→        issue_id = issue["id"]
  1154→
  1155→        worker_notes = self._gather_notes_for_worker(issue_id)
  1156→        if worker_notes:
  1157→            self.db.log_event(issue_id, agent.agent_id, "notes_injected", {"count": len(worker_notes)})
  1158→
  1159→        # NEW: delivery-based inbox injection
  1160→        inbox_section = self._prepare_inbox_for_worker(agent.agent_id, issue_id)
  1161→
  1162→        retry_context = build_retry_context(self.db, issue_id)
  1163→        branch_name = f"agent/{agent.name}"
  1164→        prompt = build_worker_prompt(
  1165→            agent_name=agent.name,
  1166→            issue=issue,
  1167→            worktree_path=agent.worktree,
  1168→            branch_name=branch_name,
  1169→            project=self.project_name,
  1170→            notes=worker_notes,
  1171→            completed_steps=completed_steps,
  1172→            retry_context=retry_context,
  1173→            inbox_section=inbox_section,
  1174→        )
  1175→
  1176→        system_prompt = build_system_prompt(
  1177→            project=self.project_name,
  1178→            agent_name=agent.name,
  1179→            worktree_path=agent.worktree,
  1180→        )
  1181→
  1182→        self.session_status_events[agent.session_id] = asyncio.Event()
  1183→        logger.debug(
  1184→            f"Created session status event for session {agent.session_id} "
  1185→            f"(agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, started_event={started_event_type})"
  1186→        )
  1187→
  1188→        await self.opencode.send_message_async(
  1189→            agent.session_id,
  1190→            parts=[{"type": "text", "text": prompt}],
  1191→            model=make_model_config(model),
  1192→            system=system_prompt,
  1193→            directory=agent.worktree,
  1194→        )
  1195→        logger.info(f"Dispatched worker prompt for session {agent.session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, model={model})")
  1196→
  1197→        self.db.log_event(issue_id, agent.agent_id, started_event_type, started_event_detail)
  1198→        monitor_task = asyncio.create_task(self.monitor_agent(agent))
  1199→        logger.debug(
  1200→            f"Started monitor task for session {agent.session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, task_id={id(monitor_task)})"
  1201→        )
  1202→
  1203→    async def _decide_completion_transition(
  1204→        self,
  1205→        agent: AgentIdentity,
  1206→        file_result: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
  1207→    ) -> CompletionDecision:
  1208→        """Decision phase for completion handling.
  1209→
  1210→        Determines the next completion transition and any payload required by
  1211→        transition side effects.
  1212→        """
  1213→        terminal_issue = self.db.get_issue(agent.issue_id)
  1214→        if terminal_issue and terminal_issue.get("status") in ("canceled", "finalized"):
  1215→            return CompletionDecision(
  1216→                transition=CompletionTransition.SKIP_TERMINAL_ISSUE,
  1217→                terminal_status=terminal_issue["status"],
  1218→            )
  1219→
  1220→        messages = await self.opencode.get_messages(agent.session_id, directory=agent.worktree)
  1221→        self._log_token_usage(agent, messages)
  1222→
  1223→        if Config.MAX_TOKENS_PER_ISSUE:
  1224→            budget_tokens = self.db.get_issue_token_total(agent.issue_id)
  1225→            if budget_tokens > Config.MAX_TOKENS_PER_ISSUE:
  1226→                logger.warning(f"Issue {agent.issue_id} exceeded token budget ({budget_tokens} > {Config.MAX_TOKENS_PER_ISSUE})")
  1227→                return CompletionDecision(
  1228→                    transition=CompletionTransition.FAIL_BUDGET,
  1229→                    budget_tokens=budget_tokens,
  1230→                    result=CompletionResult(
  1231→                        success=False,
  1232→                        reason=f"Exceeded per-issue token budget ({budget_tokens} > {Config.MAX_TOKENS_PER_ISSUE})",
  1233→                        summary=f"Terminated: per-issue token budget exceeded ({budget_tokens} tokens)",
  1234→                    ),
  1235→                )
  1236→
  1237→        # Materialize issue-following targets so we catch all pending required notes
  1238→        self.db.materialize_issue_deliveries(agent.issue_id, agent.agent_id, self.project_name)
  1239→
  1240→        # Check for required unacked notes
  1241→        unacked = self.db.get_required_unacked_deliveries(agent.agent_id, agent.issue_id)
  1242→        if unacked:
  1243→            self.db.log_event(
  1244→                agent.issue_id,
  1245→                agent.agent_id,
  1246→                "completion_blocked_unacked_notes",
  1247→                {"count": len(unacked), "delivery_ids": [d["delivery_id"] for d in unacked]},
  1248→            )
  1249→            return CompletionDecision(
  1250→                transition=CompletionTransition.FAIL_ASSESSMENT,
  1251→                result=CompletionResult(
  1252→                    success=False,
  1253→                    reason=f"Cannot complete: {len(unacked)} required note(s) not acknowledged. Acknowledge via: hive mail ack <delivery_id>",
  1254→                    summary=f"Blocked by {len(unacked)} unacknowledged required note(s)",
  1255→                ),
  1256→            )
  1257→
  1258→        result = assess_completion(messages, file_result=file_result)
  1259→        if not result.success:
  1260→            return CompletionDecision(
  1261→                transition=CompletionTransition.FAIL_ASSESSMENT,
  1262→                result=result,
  1263→            )
  1264→
  1265→        # Completion gate: block if required deliveries are not acked (spec section 9)
  1266→        # Materialize issue-following targets so we catch all pending required notes
  1267→        self.db.materialize_issue_deliveries(agent.issue_id, agent.agent_id, self.project_name)
  1268→
  1269→        # Check for required unacked notes
  1270→        unacked = self.db.get_required_unacked_deliveries(agent.agent_id, agent.issue_id)
  1271→        if unacked:
  1272→            self.db.log_event(
  1273→                agent.issue_id,
  1274→                agent.agent_id,
  1275→                "completion_blocked_unacked_notes",
  1276→                {"count": len(unacked), "delivery_ids": [d["delivery_id"] for d in unacked]},
  1277→            )
  1278→            return CompletionDecision(
  1279→                transition=CompletionTransition.FAIL_ASSESSMENT,
  1280→                result=CompletionResult(
  1281→                    success=False,
  1282→                    reason=f"Cannot complete: {len(unacked)} required note(s) not acknowledged. Acknowledge via: hive mail ack <delivery_id>",
  1283→                    summary=f"Blocked by {len(unacked)} unacknowledged required note(s)",
  1284→                ),
  1285→            )
  1286→
  1287→        has_commits = await has_diff_from_main_async(agent.worktree)
  1288→        if not has_commits:
  1289→            return CompletionDecision(
  1290→                transition=CompletionTransition.FAIL_VALIDATION_NO_DIFF,
  1291→                validation_original_summary=result.summary,
  1292→                result=CompletionResult(
  1293→                    success=False,
  1294→                    reason="No commits relative to main despite claiming success",
  1295→                    summary=result.summary,
  1296→                ),
  1297→            )
  1298→
  1299→        issue = self.db.get_issue(agent.issue_id)
  1300→        if issue and issue.get("parent_id"):
  1301→            next_step = self.db.get_next_ready_step(issue["parent_id"])
  1302→            if next_step:
  1303→                return CompletionDecision(
  1304→                    transition=CompletionTransition.SUCCESS_CYCLE_NEXT_STEP,
  1305→                    result=result,
  1306→                    next_step=next_step,
  1307→                )
  1308→
  1309→        return CompletionDecision(
  1310→            transition=CompletionTransition.SUCCESS_DONE,
  1311→            result=result,
  1312→        )
  1313→
  1314→    # Completion transition table:
  1315→    # - SKIP_TERMINAL_ISSUE      -> log agent_complete_skipped
  1316→    # - FAIL_BUDGET              -> log budget_exceeded + _handle_agent_failure
  1317→    # - FAIL_ASSESSMENT          -> _handle_agent_failure
  1318→    # - FAIL_VALIDATION_NO_DIFF  -> log validation_failed + _handle_agent_failure
  1319→    # - SUCCESS_DONE             -> update done + enqueue merge + log completed
  1320→    # - SUCCESS_CYCLE_NEXT_STEP  -> SUCCESS_DONE effects + cycle agent + skip teardown
  1321→    # - ERROR_COMPLETION_HANDLER -> log completion_error
  1322→    async def handle_agent_complete(
  1323→        self,
  1324→        agent: AgentIdentity,
  1325→        file_result: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
  1326→    ):
  1327→        """
  1328→        Handle agent completion.
  1329→
  1330→        Args:
  1331→            agent: Agent identity
  1332→            file_result: Optional parsed result from .hive-result.jsonl file.
  1333→                If provided, used directly for completion assessment (skips
  1334→                message parsing heuristics).
  1335→        """
  1336→        with self._agent_handling_scope(agent, handler_name="completion handling") as should_handle:
  1337→            if not should_handle:
  1338→                return
  1339→
  1340→            post_action = PostAction.TEARDOWN
  1341→            decision: Optional[CompletionDecision] = None
  1342→
  1343→            try:
  1344→                # Always clean up the result file if it exists
  1345→                remove_result_file(agent.worktree)
  1346→
  1347→                # Harvest notes (best-effort) — do this BEFORE the canceled check
  1348→                # so even canceled/failed workers' discoveries are saved.
  1349→                try:
  1350→                    notes_data = read_notes_file(agent.worktree)
  1351→                    if notes_data:
  1352→                        for note in notes_data:
  1353→                            self.db.add_note(
  1354→                                issue_id=agent.issue_id,
  1355→                                agent_id=agent.agent_id,
  1356→                                content=note.get("content", ""),
  1357→                                category=note.get("category", "discovery"),
  1358→                                project=self.project_name,
  1359→                            )
  1360→                        self.db.log_event(agent.issue_id, agent.agent_id, "notes_harvested", {"count": len(notes_data)})
  1361→                        logger.info(f"Harvested {len(notes_data)} notes from {agent.name}")
  1362→                except Exception as e:
  1363→                    logger.warning(f"Failed to harvest notes from {agent.name}: {e}")
  1364→                finally:
  1365→                    remove_notes_file(agent.worktree)
  1366→
  1367→                decision = await self._decide_completion_transition(agent, file_result=file_result)
  1368→
  1369→                match decision.transition:
  1370→                    case CompletionTransition.SKIP_TERMINAL_ISSUE:
  1371→                        status = decision.terminal_status or "unknown"
  1372→                        self.db.log_event(
  1373→                            agent.issue_id,
  1374→                            agent.agent_id,
  1375→                            "agent_complete_skipped",
  1376→                            {"reason": f"issue already {status}, cleaning up session"},
  1377→                        )
  1378→
  1379→                    case CompletionTransition.FAIL_BUDGET:
  1380→                        self.db.log_event(
  1381→                            agent.issue_id,
  1382→                            agent.agent_id,
  1383→                            "budget_exceeded",
  1384→                            {"issue_tokens": decision.budget_tokens, "limit": Config.MAX_TOKENS_PER_ISSUE},
  1385→                        )
  1386→                        if decision.result is not None:
  1387→                            await self._handle_agent_failure(agent, decision.result)
  1388→
  1389→                    case CompletionTransition.FAIL_VALIDATION_NO_DIFF:
  1390→                        self.db.log_event(
  1391→                            agent.issue_id,
  1392→                            agent.agent_id,
  1393→                            "validation_failed",
  1394→                            {
  1395→                                "reason": "No commits relative to main despite claiming success",
  1396→                                "original_summary": decision.validation_original_summary,
  1397→                            },
  1398→                        )
  1399→                        if decision.result is not None:
  1400→                            await self._handle_agent_failure(agent, decision.result)
  1401→
  1402→                    case CompletionTransition.FAIL_ASSESSMENT:
  1403→                        if decision.result is not None:
  1404→                            await self._handle_agent_failure(agent, decision.result)
  1405→
  1406→                    case CompletionTransition.SUCCESS_DONE | CompletionTransition.SUCCESS_CYCLE_NEXT_STEP:
  1407→                        if decision.result is None:
  1408→                            raise RuntimeError("Missing completion result for success transition")
  1409→
  1410→                        # Mark issue as done.
  1411→                        self.db.update_issue_status(agent.issue_id, "done")
  1412→
  1413→                        # Get commit hash if available.
  1414→                        commit_hash = decision.result.git_commit or get_commit_hash(agent.worktree)
  1415→
  1416→                        # Extract test_command from worker's file_result.
  1417→                        test_command = file_result.get("test_command") if file_result else None
  1418→
  1419→                        # Enqueue to merge queue.
  1420→                        self.db.conn.execute(
  1421→                            """
  1422→                            INSERT INTO merge_queue (issue_id, agent_id, project, worktree, branch_name, test_command)
  1423→                            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
  1424→                            """,
  1425→                            (
  1426→                                agent.issue_id,
  1427→                                agent.agent_id,
  1428→                                self.project_name,
  1429→                                agent.worktree,
  1430→                                f"agent/{agent.name}",
  1431→                                test_command,
  1432→                            ),
  1433→                        )
  1434→                        self.db.conn.commit()
  1435→
  1436→                        # Get agent model from database.
  1437→                        agent_row = self.db.get_agent(agent.agent_id)
  1438→                        model = agent_row["model"] if agent_row else None
  1439→
  1440→                        self.db.log_event(
  1441→                            agent.issue_id,
  1442→                            agent.agent_id,
  1443→                            "completed",
  1444→                            {
  1445→                                "summary": decision.result.summary,
  1446→                                "commit": commit_hash,
  1447→                                "artifacts": decision.result.artifacts,
  1448→                                "model": model,
  1449→                            },
  1450→                        )
  1451→
  1452→                        if decision.transition == CompletionTransition.SUCCESS_CYCLE_NEXT_STEP:
  1453→                            if decision.next_step is None:
  1454→                                raise RuntimeError("Missing next step for cycle transition")
  1455→                            await self.cycle_agent_to_next_step(agent, decision.next_step)
  1456→                            if agent.agent_id in self.active_agents:
  1457→                                post_action = PostAction.CONTINUE_AGENT
  1458→
  1459→                    case _:
  1460→                        raise RuntimeError(f"Unhandled completion transition: {decision.transition}")
  1461→
  1462→            except Exception as e:
  1463→                transition = decision.transition.value if decision else CompletionTransition.ERROR_COMPLETION_HANDLER.value
  1464→                self.db.log_event(
  1465→                    agent.issue_id,
  1466→                    agent.agent_id,
  1467→                    "completion_error",
  1468→                    {"error": str(e), "transition": transition},
  1469→                )
  1470→            finally:
  1471→                if post_action == PostAction.TEARDOWN:
  1472→                    await self._teardown_agent(agent)
  1473→
  1474→    def _choose_escalation(self, issue_id: str) -> EscalationDecision:
  1475→        """Decide escalation tier based on anomaly/retry/switch counts."""
  1476→        if Config.ANOMALY_FAILURE_THRESHOLD and Config.ANOMALY_WINDOW_MINUTES:
  1477→            recent_failures = self.db.count_events_since_minutes(issue_id, "incomplete", Config.ANOMALY_WINDOW_MINUTES)
  1478→            if recent_failures >= Config.ANOMALY_FAILURE_THRESHOLD:
  1479→                return EscalationDecision.ANOMALY_ESCALATE
  1480→
  1481→        retry_count = self.db.count_events_by_type(issue_id, "retry")
  1482→        if retry_count < Config.MAX_RETRIES:
  1483→            return EscalationDecision.RETRY
  1484→
  1485→        agent_switch_count = self.db.count_events_by_type(issue_id, "agent_switch")
  1486→        if agent_switch_count < Config.MAX_AGENT_SWITCHES:
  1487→            return EscalationDecision.AGENT_SWITCH
  1488→
  1489→        return EscalationDecision.ESCALATE
  1490→
  1491→    async def _handle_agent_failure(self, agent: AgentIdentity, result: CompletionResult):
  1492→        """State machine for failure routing: retry -> agent switch -> escalate."""
  1493→        issue_id = agent.issue_id
  1494→
  1495→        # Log the failure first so anomaly checks see this occurrence.
  1496→        agent_row = self.db.get_agent(agent.agent_id)
  1497→        model = agent_row["model"] if agent_row else None
  1498→        self.db.log_event(
  1499→            issue_id,
  1500→            agent.agent_id,
  1501→            "incomplete",
  1502→            {"reason": result.reason, "summary": result.summary, "model": model},
  1503→        )
  1504→
  1505→        decision = self._choose_escalation(issue_id)
  1506→
  1507→        if decision == EscalationDecision.ANOMALY_ESCALATE:
  1508→            recent_failures = self.db.count_events_since_minutes(issue_id, "incomplete", Config.ANOMALY_WINDOW_MINUTES)
  1509→            logger.warning(f"Anomaly: {recent_failures} failures on {issue_id} in {Config.ANOMALY_WINDOW_MINUTES}m — auto-escalating")
  1510→            self.db.update_issue_status(issue_id, "escalated")
  1511→            self.db.log_event(
  1512→                issue_id,
  1513→                agent.agent_id,
  1514→                "escalated",
  1515→                {
  1516→                    "reason": "Anomaly detection: rapid repeated failures",
  1517→                    "recent_failures": recent_failures,
  1518→                    "window_minutes": Config.ANOMALY_WINDOW_MINUTES,
  1519→                    "final_failure_reason": result.reason,
  1520→                },
  1521→            )
  1522→            return
  1523→
  1524→        if decision == EscalationDecision.RETRY:
  1525→            retry_count = self.db.count_events_by_type(issue_id, "retry")
  1526→            self._release_issue(issue_id)
  1527→            self.db.log_event(
  1528→                issue_id,
  1529→                agent.agent_id,
  1530→                "retry",
  1531→                {"retry_count": retry_count + 1, "reason": result.reason, "previous_agent": agent.name},
  1532→            )
  1533→            logger.info(f"Retrying issue {issue_id} (attempt {retry_count + 1}/{Config.MAX_RETRIES})")
  1534→            return
  1535→
  1536→        if decision == EscalationDecision.AGENT_SWITCH:
  1537→            agent_switch_count = self.db.count_events_by_type(issue_id, "agent_switch")
  1538→            self._release_issue(issue_id)
  1539→            self.db.log_event(
  1540→                issue_id,
  1541→                agent.agent_id,
  1542→                "agent_switch",
  1543→                {"switch_count": agent_switch_count + 1, "reason": result.reason, "previous_agent": agent.name, "model": model},
  1544→            )
  1545→            logger.info(f"Switching agent for issue {issue_id} (switch {agent_switch_count + 1}/{Config.MAX_AGENT_SWITCHES})")
  1546→            return
  1547→
  1548→        retry_count = self.db.count_events_by_type(issue_id, "retry")
  1549→        agent_switch_count = self.db.count_events_by_type(issue_id, "agent_switch")
  1550→        self.db.update_issue_status(issue_id, "escalated")
  1551→        self.db.log_event(
  1552→            issue_id,
  1553→            agent.agent_id,
  1554→            "escalated",
  1555→            {
  1556→                "reason": "Exhausted all retry and agent switch attempts",
  1557→                "final_failure_reason": result.reason,
  1558→                "total_retries": retry_count,
  1559→                "total_agent_switches": agent_switch_count,
  1560→            },
  1561→        )
  1562→        logger.warning(f"Escalating issue {issue_id} to human intervention after {retry_count} retries and {agent_switch_count} agent switches")
  1563→
  1564→    async def cycle_agent_to_next_step(self, agent: AgentIdentity, next_step: Dict[str, Any]):
  1565→        """
  1566→        Cycle an agent to the next step in a epic.
  1567→
  1568→        Args:
  1569→            agent: Current agent identity
  1570→            next_step: Next step issue dict
  1571→        """
  1572→        # Abort and delete current session (not just abort — delete prevents leaked sessions)
  1573→        old_session_id = agent.session_id
  1574→        logger.info(f"Cycling agent {agent.agent_id} to next epic step; cleaning up old session {old_session_id} (next_step={next_step['id']})")
  1575→        await self.opencode.cleanup_session(old_session_id, directory=agent.worktree)
  1576→
  1577→        # Claim the next step
  1578→        claimed = self.db.claim_issue(next_step["id"], agent.agent_id)
  1579→        if not claimed:
  1580→            # Someone else claimed it, release agent
  1581→            if agent.agent_id in self.active_agents:
  1582→                self._unregister_agent(agent.agent_id)
  1583→            return
  1584→
  1585→        # Resolve model for the next step: next_step.model > Config.WORKER_MODEL > Config.DEFAULT_MODEL
  1586→        model = next_step.get("model") or Config.WORKER_MODEL or Config.DEFAULT_MODEL
  1587→
  1588→        # Create new session (same worktree)
  1589→        new_session_id = None  # Track for cleanup on failure
  1590→        try:
  1591→            session = await self.opencode.create_session(
  1592→                directory=agent.worktree,
  1593→                title=f"{agent.name}: {next_step['title']}",
  1594→                permissions=WORKER_PERMISSIONS,
  1595→            )
  1596→            new_session_id = session["id"]
  1597→
  1598→            # Update agent
  1599→            self.db.conn.execute(
  1600→                """
  1601→                UPDATE agents
  1602→                SET session_id = ?,
  1603→                    current_issue = ?,
  1604→                    lease_expires_at = datetime('now', '+{} seconds'),
  1605→                    last_progress_at = datetime('now')
  1606→                WHERE id = ?
  1607→                """.format(Config.LEASE_DURATION),
  1608→                (new_session_id, next_step["id"], agent.agent_id),
  1609→            )
  1610→            self.db.conn.commit()
  1611→
  1612→            self._reassign_agent_context(agent, session_id=new_session_id, issue_id=next_step["id"])
  1613→
  1614→            # Gather completed steps for context
  1615→            completed_steps = None
  1616→            if next_step.get("parent_id"):
  1617→                completed_issues = self.db.get_completed_epic_steps(next_step["parent_id"])
  1618→                completed_steps = [f"{s['title']}: {(s.get('description') or '')[:100]}" for s in completed_issues]
  1619→
  1620→            await self._dispatch_worker_to_issue(
  1621→                agent=agent,
  1622→                issue=next_step,
  1623→                model=model,
  1624→                completed_steps=completed_steps,
  1625→                started_event_type="session_cycled",
  1626→                started_event_detail={
  1627→                    "new_session_id": new_session_id,
  1628→                    "step_title": next_step["title"],
  1629→                    "prompt_version": get_prompt_version("worker"),
  1630→                },
  1631→            )
  1632→
  1633→        except Exception as e:
  1634→            self.db.log_event(
  1635→                next_step["id"],
  1636→                agent.agent_id,
  1637→                "session_cycle_error",
  1638→                {"error": str(e)},
  1639→            )
  1640→            # Clean up the new session if it was created (best-effort —
  1641→            # don't let cleanup failure prevent _unregister_agent below)
  1642→            if new_session_id:
  1643→                logger.warning(
  1644→                    f"Cycle failed after creating session {new_session_id}; cleaning up (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={next_step['id']})"
  1645→                )
  1646→                await self._best_effort_cleanup(
  1647→                    "cycle_session_cleanup",
  1648→                    self.opencode.cleanup_session(new_session_id, directory=agent.worktree),
  1649→                )
  1650→            # Release agent
  1651→            if agent.agent_id in self.active_agents:
  1652→                self._unregister_agent(agent.agent_id)
  1653→
  1654→    async def handle_stalled_agent(self, agent: AgentIdentity):
  1655→        """
  1656→        Handle a stalled agent (lease expired).
  1657→
  1658→        Routes through the retry escalation chain so that repeatedly
  1659→        stalling issues eventually get escalated instead of looping
  1660→        forever.
  1661→
  1662→        Args:
  1663→            agent: Agent identity
  1664→        """
  1665→        # Stalled transition table:
  1666→        # - FAIL_STALLED_IN_PROGRESS -> mark failed + escalate via _handle_agent_failure + teardown
  1667→        # - FAIL_STALLED_TERMINAL    -> mark failed + teardown (no escalation)
  1668→
  1669→        with self._agent_handling_scope(agent, handler_name="stall handling") as should_handle:
  1670→            if not should_handle:
  1671→                return
  1672→
  1673→            stalled_transition = StalledTransition.FAIL_STALLED_TERMINAL
  1674→            try:
  1675→                self.db.log_event(
  1676→                    agent.issue_id,
  1677→                    agent.agent_id,
  1678→                    "stalled",
  1679→                    {"lease_expired": True},
  1680→                )
  1681→
  1682→                # Mark agent as failed so it's not picked up again
  1683→                self._mark_agent_failed(agent.agent_id)
  1684→
  1685→                # Route through escalation chain (retry → agent_switch → escalate)
  1686→                # instead of unconditionally resetting to open, which caused an
  1687→                # infinite spawn loop for issues whose workers always stall.
  1688→                current_issue = self.db.get_issue(agent.issue_id)
  1689→                if current_issue and current_issue.get("status") == "in_progress":
  1690→                    stalled_transition = StalledTransition.FAIL_STALLED_IN_PROGRESS
  1691→                    stall_result = CompletionResult(
  1692→                        success=False,
  1693→                        reason="Agent stalled (lease expired, no activity)",
  1694→                        summary="Worker became unresponsive",
  1695→                    )
  1696→                    await self._handle_agent_failure(agent, stall_result)
  1697→            finally:
  1698→                logger.debug(f"Stall transition for {agent.name}: {stalled_transition.value}")
  1699→                await self._teardown_agent(agent, remove_worktree=True)
  1700→
  1701→    async def _check_opencode_health(self) -> bool:
  1702→        """
  1703→        Check if OpenCode is healthy by listing sessions via the client.
  1704→
  1705→        Reuses the existing OpenCodeClient (and its connection pool / auth)
  1706→        instead of creating a throwaway aiohttp.ClientSession per call.
  1707→
  1708→        Returns:
  1709→            True if OpenCode is healthy, False otherwise
  1710→        """
  1711→        try:
  1712→            await self.opencode.list_sessions()
  1713→            return True
  1714→        except Exception:
  1715→            return False
  1716→
  1717→    def _is_opencode_error(self, exception: Exception) -> bool:
  1718→        """
  1719→        Determine if an exception indicates OpenCode connectivity issues.
  1720→
  1721→        Args:
  1722→            exception: Exception to examine
  1723→
  1724→        Returns:
  1725→            True if this suggests OpenCode is unavailable
  1726→        """
  1727→        error_msg = str(exception).lower()
  1728→
  1729→        # Common connectivity issues
  1730→        if any(
  1731→            phrase in error_msg
  1732→            for phrase in [
  1733→                "connection refused",
  1734→                "connection failed",
  1735→                "timeout",
  1736→                "server error",
  1737→                "network",
  1738→                "unreachable",
  1739→                "500",
  1740→                "502",
  1741→                "503",
  1742→                "504",
  1743→            ]
  1744→        ):
  1745→            return True
  1746→
  1747→        # HTTP status code 5xx errors
  1748→        if hasattr(exception, "status") and hasattr(exception.status, "__ge__"):
  1749→            return exception.status >= 500
  1750→
  1751→        return False
  1752→
  1753→    async def _enter_degraded_mode(self, error_reason: str):
  1754→        """
  1755→        Enter degraded mode due to OpenCode unavailability.
  1756→
  1757→        Args:
  1758→            error_reason: Description of the error that caused degraded mode
  1759→        """
  1760→        if self._opencode_healthy:  # Only log the first time we enter degraded mode
  1761→            self._opencode_healthy = False
  1762→            self._degraded_since = datetime.now()
  1763→            self._backoff_delay = 5  # Reset backoff
  1764→
  1765→            self.db.log_system_event("opencode_degraded", {"reason": error_reason, "timestamp": self._degraded_since.isoformat()})
  1766→            logger.warning(f"Entering degraded mode: {error_reason}")
  1767→
  1768→    async def check_stalled_agents(self):
  1769→        """Check for stalled agents owned by THIS daemon and handle them.
  1770→
  1771→        Only checks agents in self.active_agents (in-memory). This prevents
  1772→        a newly restarted daemon from interfering with stale DB rows left
  1773→        by a previous daemon instance.
  1774→
  1775→        Now enhanced with session status inspection to avoid false positives
  1776→        from missed SSE events.
  1777→        """
  1778→        if not self.active_agents:
  1779→            return
  1780→
  1781→        # Check each active agent against the DB lease
  1782→        stalled = []
  1783→        for agent_id, agent in list(self.active_agents.items()):
  1784→            try:
  1785→                cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
  1786→                    """
  1787→                    SELECT lease_expires_at
  1788→                    FROM agents
  1789→                    WHERE id = ? AND status = 'working'
  1790→                      AND lease_expires_at < datetime('now')
  1791→                    """,
  1792→                    (agent_id,),
  1793→                )
  1794→                row = cursor.fetchone()
  1795→                if row:
  1796→                    stalled.append(agent)
  1797→            except Exception:
  1798→                pass
  1799→
  1800→        # For stalled agents, check OpenCode session status before handling
  1801→        for agent in stalled:
  1802→            await self._handle_stalled_with_session_check(agent)
  1803→
  1804→    async def _handle_stalled_with_session_check(self, agent: AgentIdentity):
  1805→        """Handle stalled agent with OpenCode session status verification.
  1806→
  1807→        Checks if the session is actually idle (completion missed due to SSE failure)
  1808→        or busy (false positive, extend lease) before falling back to handle_stalled_agent.
  1809→        """
  1810→        try:
  1811→            # Query OpenCode for actual session status
  1812→            status = await self.opencode.get_session_status(agent.session_id, directory=agent.worktree)
  1813→
  1814→            if status["type"] == "idle":
  1815→                # Session finished but we missed the SSE completion event
  1816→                self.db.log_event(agent.issue_id, agent.agent_id, "missed_completion", {"session_status": "idle", "reason": "sse_missed"})
  1817→                await self.handle_agent_complete(agent)
  1818→                return
  1819→
  1820→            elif status["type"] == "busy":
  1821→                # Session still active - check if we've already extended the lease
  1822→                cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
  1823→                    """
  1824→                    SELECT 1 FROM events 
  1825→                    WHERE agent_id = ? AND event_type = 'lease_extended'
  1826→                    AND created_at > datetime('now', '-{} seconds')
  1827→                    """.format(Config.LEASE_DURATION),
  1828→                    (agent.agent_id,),
  1829→                )
  1830→
  1831→                if cursor.fetchone():
  1832→                    # Already extended lease in current period - treat as truly stalled
  1833→                    await self.handle_stalled_agent(agent)
  1834→                else:
  1835→                    # First extension - extend the lease
  1836→                    self.db.conn.execute(
  1837→                        "UPDATE agents SET lease_expires_at = datetime('now', '+{} seconds') WHERE id = ?".format(Config.LEASE_DURATION),
  1838→                        (agent.agent_id,),
  1839→                    )
  1840→                    self.db.conn.commit()
  1841→
  1842→                    self.db.log_event(
  1843→                        agent.issue_id,
  1844→                        agent.agent_id,
  1845→                        "lease_extended",
  1846→                        {"session_status": "busy", "new_expires_at": "now+{}s".format(Config.LEASE_DURATION)},
  1847→                    )
  1848→                return
  1849→
  1850→        except Exception as e:
  1851→            # OpenCode API failure - fall back to original behavior
  1852→            self.db.log_event(agent.issue_id, agent.agent_id, "session_check_failed", {"error": str(e), "fallback": "handle_stalled_agent"})
  1853→
  1854→        # Default fallback behavior
  1855→        await self.handle_stalled_agent(agent)
  1856→
  1857→    def _on_merge_task_done(self, task: asyncio.Task):
  1858→        """Handle merge_processor_loop task completion/failure.
  1859→
  1860→        If the task died unexpectedly and the orchestrator is still running,
  1861→        auto-restart the merge processor loop.
  1862→        """
  1863→        if task.cancelled():
  1864→            logger.info("Merge processor loop was cancelled")
  1865→            return
  1866→
  1867→        exception = task.exception()
  1868→        if exception:
  1869→            logger.error(f"Merge processor loop died with exception: {exception}")
  1870→            if self.running:
  1871→                logger.info("Auto-restarting merge processor loop")
  1872→                new_task = asyncio.create_task(self.merge_processor_loop())
  1873→                new_task.add_done_callback(self._on_merge_task_done)
  1874→
  1875→    async def merge_processor_loop(self):
  1876→        """
  1877→        Background loop to process the merge queue.
  1878→
  1879→        Runs on MERGE_POLL_INTERVAL, processes one merge at a time.
  1880→        Includes periodic health checks for the refinery session.
  1881→        """
  1882→        health_check_counter = 0
  1883→
  1884→        while self.running:
  1885→            try:
  1886→                if Config.MERGE_QUEUE_ENABLED:
  1887→                    await self.merge_processor.process_queue_once()
  1888→
  1889→                # Health check every 6 iterations (~60s at 10s poll interval)
  1890→                health_check_counter += 1
  1891→                if health_check_counter >= 6:
  1892→                    health_check_counter = 0
  1893→                    await self.merge_processor.health_check()
  1894→
  1895→            except Exception as e:
  1896→                logger.error(f"Error in merge processor: {e}")
  1897→            await asyncio.sleep(Config.MERGE_POLL_INTERVAL)
  1898→
  1899→    async def permission_unblocker_loop(self):
  1900→        """
  1901→        Safety-net loop to auto-resolve pending permission requests based on policy.
  1902→
  1903→        Now runs at longer intervals since SSE events handle real-time permissions.
  1904→        This serves as a safety net for SSE reconnection gaps or edge cases.
  1905→        """
  1906→        while self.running:
  1907→            try:
  1908→                # Very slow if no active agents
  1909→                if len(self.active_agents) == 0:
  1910→                    await asyncio.sleep(30)
  1911→                    continue
  1912→
  1913→                # Get pending permissions
  1914→                pending = await self.opencode.get_pending_permissions()
  1915→
  1916→                for perm in pending:
  1917→                    decision = self.evaluate_permission_policy(perm)
  1918→                    if decision:
  1919→                        # Auto-resolve based on policy
  1920→                        await self.opencode.reply_permission(perm["id"], reply=decision)
  1921→
  1922→                        self._log_permission_resolved(perm, decision)
  1923→
  1924→                # Safety net only - SSE handles real-time permissions
  1925→                await asyncio.sleep(Config.PERMISSION_SAFETY_NET_INTERVAL)
  1926→
  1927→            except Exception as e:
  1928→                logger.error(f"Error in permission unblocker: {e}")
  1929→                await asyncio.sleep(Config.PERMISSION_SAFETY_NET_INTERVAL)
  1930→
  1931→    def evaluate_permission_policy(self, perm: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]:
  1932→        """
  1933→        Apply policy rules to decide allow/deny.
  1934→
  1935→        Args:
  1936→            perm: Permission request dict from OpenCode
  1937→
  1938→        Returns:
  1939→            "once", "always", or None if no rule matches
  1940→        """
  1941→        permission = perm.get("permission")
  1942→
  1943→        # Session-level permissions handle most cases (set at session creation).
  1944→        # This catches runtime permission requests that slip through.
  1945→
  1946→        # Workers should never ask questions or enter plan mode
  1947→        if permission in ("question", "plan_enter", "plan_exit"):
  1948→            return "reject"
  1949→
  1950→        # Workers should never leave their worktree
  1951→        if permission == "external_directory":
  1952→            return "reject"
  1953→
  1954→        # Allow standard tool usage within the session's directory scope
  1955→        if permission in ("read", "edit", "write", "bash"):
  1956→            return "once"
  1957→
  1958→        # Unknown permission - let it block (human reviews)
  1959→        return None
  1960→

388→    def get_ready_queue(self, project: Optional[str] = None, limit: Optional[int] = None) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   389→        """
   390→        Query for ready work items.
   391→
   392→        Returns issues that are:
   393→        - status = 'open'
   394→        - assignee IS NULL
   395→        - All blocking dependencies are resolved (done/finalized/canceled)
   396→
   397→        Args:
   398→            project: Filter by project (optional)
   399→            limit: Maximum number of items to return
   400→
   401→        Returns:
   402→            List of issue dicts, ordered by priority then creation time
   403→        """
   404→        query = """
   405→            SELECT i.*
   406→            FROM issues i
   407→            WHERE i.status = 'open'
   408→              AND i.assignee IS NULL
   409→              AND i.type != 'epic'
   410→              AND NOT EXISTS (
   411→                SELECT 1 FROM dependencies d
   412→                JOIN issues blocker ON d.depends_on = blocker.id
   413→                WHERE d.issue_id = i.id
   414→                  AND d.type = 'blocks'
   415→                  AND blocker.status NOT IN ('done', 'finalized', 'canceled')
   416→              )
   417→        """
   418→
   419→        params = []
   420→        if project is not None:
   421→            query += " AND i.project = ?"
   422→            params.append(project)
   423→
   424→        query += " ORDER BY i.priority ASC, i.created_at ASC"
   425→
   426→        if limit:
   427→            query += f" LIMIT {limit}"
   428→
   429→        cursor = self.conn.execute(query, params)
   430→        return [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   431→
   432→    def claim_issue(self, issue_id: str, agent_id: str) -> bool:
   433→        """
   434→        Atomically claim an issue using CAS (Compare-And-Set).
   435→
   436→        Only succeeds if the issue is unclaimed AND all blocking dependencies
   437→        are resolved. This prevents a race where the orchestrator grabs an issue
   438→        from the ready queue before its dependencies have been wired.
   439→
   440→        Args:
   441→            issue_id: ID of issue to claim
   442→            agent_id: ID of agent claiming the issue
   443→
   444→        Returns:
   445→            True if claim successful, False if already claimed or blocked
   446→        """
   447→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   448→            cursor = conn.execute(
   449→                """
   450→                UPDATE issues
   451→                SET assignee = ?,
   452→                    status = 'in_progress',
   453→                    updated_at = datetime('now')
   454→                WHERE id = ?
   455→                  AND assignee IS NULL
   456→                  AND NOT EXISTS (
   457→                    SELECT 1 FROM dependencies d
   458→                    JOIN issues blocker ON d.depends_on = blocker.id
   459→                    WHERE d.issue_id = ?
   460→                      AND d.type = 'blocks'
   461→                      AND blocker.status NOT IN ('done', 'finalized', 'canceled')
   462→                  )
   463→                """,
   464→                (agent_id, issue_id, issue_id),
   465→            )
   466→
   467→            success = cursor.rowcount == 1

1→"""Git worktree management for agent sandboxes."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→import os
     5→import shutil
     6→import subprocess
     7→import time
     8→from pathlib import Path
     9→from typing import Optional
    10→
    11→
    12→class GitWorktreeError(Exception):
    13→    """Exception raised for git worktree operations."""
    14→
    15→    pass
    16→
    17→
    18→def create_worktree(project_path: str, agent_name: str, base_branch: str = "main") -> str:
    19→    """
    20→    Create a git worktree for an agent.
    21→
    22→    Args:
    23→        project_path: Path to the main git repository
    24→        agent_name: Name of the agent (used for branch and directory name)
    25→        base_branch: Base branch to branch from (default: main)
    26→
    27→    Returns:
    28→        Path to the created worktree directory
    29→
    30→    Raises:
    31→        GitWorktreeError: If worktree creation fails
    32→    """
    33→    project_path = Path(project_path).resolve()
    34→
    35→    if not (project_path / ".git").exists():
    36→        raise GitWorktreeError(f"Not a git repository: {project_path}")
    37→
    38→    # Worktree directory: <project>/.worktrees/<agent_name>
    39→    worktree_dir = project_path / ".worktrees" / agent_name
    40→    worktree_dir.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    41→
    42→    # Branch name: agent/<agent_name>
    43→    branch_name = f"agent/{agent_name}"
    44→
    45→    # Retry with backoff — concurrent worktree creation can transiently fail
    46→    # with "invalid reference: main" when git ref resolution hits contention.
    47→    # TODO: Investigate a proper fix for git ref contention instead of this
    48→    # sleep-and-retry hack. It's unclear whether longer sleeps actually help
    49→    # or if the root cause is something else entirely (e.g. packed-refs
    50→    # rewriting, loose ref gc, or worktree metadata races).
    51→    max_retries = 4
    52→    for attempt in range(max_retries):
    53→        try:
    54→            subprocess.run(
    55→                [
    56→                    "git",
    57→                    "worktree",
    58→                    "add",
    59→                    "-b",
    60→                    branch_name,
    61→                    str(worktree_dir),
    62→                    base_branch,
    63→                ],
    64→                cwd=str(project_path),
    65→                check=True,
    66→                capture_output=True,
    67→                text=True,
    68→            )
    69→            return str(worktree_dir)
    70→
    71→        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
    72→            is_transient = "invalid reference" in e.stderr or "index.lock" in e.stderr
    73→            if is_transient and attempt < max_retries - 1:
    74→                time.sleep(1.0 * (attempt + 1))
    75→                continue
    76→            raise GitWorktreeError(f"Failed to create worktree: {e.stderr}") from e
    77→
    78→
    79→def remove_worktree(worktree_path: str) -> bool:
    80→    """Remove a git worktree."""
    81→    abs_path = os.path.abspath(worktree_path)
    82→    if not os.path.exists(abs_path):
    83→        return False
    84→    try:
    85→        subprocess.run(
    86→            ["git", "worktree", "remove", "--force", abs_path],
    87→            capture_output=True,
    88→            text=True,
    89→            check=True,
    90→            cwd=os.path.dirname(abs_path),  # any valid directory works
    91→        )
    92→        return True
    93→    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
    94→        # Fallback: force remove if worktree is dirty
    95→        try:
    96→            shutil.rmtree(abs_path, ignore_errors=True)
    97→            subprocess.run(["git", "worktree", "prune"], capture_output=True, cwd=os.path.dirname(abs_path))
    98→            return True
    99→        except Exception:
   100→            return False
   101→
   102→
   103→def delete_branch(project_path: str, branch_name: str, force: bool = False):
   104→    """
   105→    Delete a git branch.
   106→
   107→    Args:
   108→        project_path: Path to the git repository
   109→        branch_name: Name of the branch to delete
   110→        force: Force deletion even if not fully merged
   111→
   112→    Raises:
   113→        GitWorktreeError: If branch deletion fails
   114→    """
   115→    try:
   116→        cmd = ["git", "branch", "-D" if force else "-d", branch_name]
   117→        subprocess.run(
   118→            cmd,
   119→            cwd=str(project_path),
   120→            check=True,
   121→            capture_output=True,
   122→            text=True,
   123→        )
   124→    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
   125→        raise GitWorktreeError(f"Failed to delete branch: {e.stderr}") from e
   126→
   127→
   128→def rebase_onto_main(worktree_path: str, main_branch: str = "main") -> bool:
   129→    """
   130→    Rebase the worktree branch onto the latest main branch.
   131→
   132→    Args:
   133→        worktree_path: Path to the worktree
   134→        main_branch: Branch to rebase onto (default: main)
   135→
   136→    Returns:
   137→        True if rebase succeeded cleanly, False if conflicts occurred.
   138→
   139→    Raises:
   140→        GitWorktreeError: On unexpected git failures (not conflicts)
   141→    """
   142→    try:
   143→        subprocess.run(
   144→            ["git", "fetch", "origin", main_branch],
   145→            cwd=str(worktree_path),
   146→            check=True,
   147→            capture_output=True,
   148→            text=True,
   149→        )
   150→    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
   151→        pass  # fetch may fail if no remote, continue with local
   152→
   153→    try:
   154→        subprocess.run(
   155→            ["git", "rebase", main_branch],
   156→            cwd=str(worktree_path),
   157→            check=True,
   158→            capture_output=True,
   159→            text=True,
   160→        )
   161→        return True
   162→    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
   163→        # Conflict detection: rebase exits non-zero on conflicts
   164→        if "CONFLICT" in e.stdout or "conflict" in e.stderr.lower() or "could not apply" in e.stderr.lower():
   165→            return False
   166→        # Also treat merge failures as conflicts (rebase couldn't apply)
   167→        if e.returncode in (1, 128):
   168→            return False
   169→        raise GitWorktreeError(f"Rebase failed unexpectedly: {e.stderr}") from e
   170→
   171→
   172→def abort_rebase(worktree_path: str):
   173→    """
   174→    Abort an in-progress rebase.
   175→
   176→    Args:
   177→        worktree_path: Path to the worktree
   178→
   179→    Raises:
   180→        GitWorktreeError: If abort fails
   181→    """
   182→    try:
   183→        subprocess.run(
   184→            ["git", "rebase", "--abort"],
   185→            cwd=str(worktree_path),
   186→            check=True,
   187→            capture_output=True,
   188→            text=True,
   189→        )
   190→    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
   191→        # If no rebase in progress, that's fine
   192→        if "no rebase in progress" in e.stderr.lower():
   193→            return
   194→        raise GitWorktreeError(f"Failed to abort rebase: {e.stderr}") from e
   195→
   196→
   197→def merge_to_main(project_path: str, branch_name: str, main_branch: str = "main"):
   198→    """
   199→    Fast-forward merge a branch into main from the main project repo.
   200→
   201→    This runs in the MAIN repo (not a worktree). The branch should already
   202→    be rebased onto main so ff-only succeeds.
   203→
   204→    Args:
   205→        project_path: Path to the main git repository
   206→        branch_name: Branch to merge (e.g. "agent/worker-abc123")
   207→        main_branch: Target branch (default: main)
   208→
   209→    Raises:
   210→        GitWorktreeError: If merge fails
   211→    """
   212→    project_path = Path(project_path).resolve()
   213→
   214→    try:
   215→        # Ensure we're on main
   216→        subprocess.run(
   217→            ["git", "checkout", main_branch],
   218→            cwd=str(project_path),
   219→            check=True,
   220→            capture_output=True,
   221→            text=True,
   222→        )
   223→
   224→        # Fast-forward only merge
   225→        subprocess.run(
   226→            ["git", "merge", "--ff-only", branch_name],
   227→            cwd=str(project_path),
   228→            check=True,
   229→            capture_output=True,
   230→            text=True,
   231→        )
   232→    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
   233→        raise GitWorktreeError(f"Failed to merge {branch_name} to {main_branch}: {e.stderr}") from e
   234→
   235→
   236→def get_worktree_dirty_status(project_path: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
   237→    """
   238→    Check whether a repository worktree has local changes.
   239→
   240→    Args:
   241→        project_path: Path to the git repository worktree
   242→
   243→    Returns:
   244→        Tuple of (is_dirty, porcelain_output)
   245→
   246→    Raises:
   247→        GitWorktreeError: If git status cannot be read
   248→    """
   249→    project_path = Path(project_path).resolve()
   250→    try:
   251→        result = subprocess.run(
   252→            ["git", "status", "--porcelain", "--untracked-files=no"],
   253→            cwd=str(project_path),
   254→            check=True,
   255→            capture_output=True,
   256→            text=True,
   257→        )
   258→        output = result.stdout.strip()
   259→        return (bool(output), output)
   260→    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
   261→        raise GitWorktreeError(f"Failed to check worktree status: {e.stderr}") from e
   262→
   263→
   264→def run_command_in_worktree(worktree_path: str, cmd: str, timeout: int = 300) -> tuple:
   265→    """
   266→    Run an arbitrary shell command in a worktree.
   267→
   268→    Useful for running test commands, linters, etc.
   269→
   270→    Args:
   271→        worktree_path: Path to the worktree
   272→        cmd: Shell command to run
   273→        timeout: Timeout in seconds (default: 300)
   274→
   275→    Returns:
   276→        Tuple of (success: bool, output: str) where output is combined stdout+stderr
   277→    """
   278→    try:
   279→        result = subprocess.run(
   280→            cmd,
   281→            shell=True,
   282→            cwd=str(worktree_path),
   283→            capture_output=True,
   284→            text=True,
   285→            timeout=timeout,
   286→        )
   287→        output = result.stdout + result.stderr
   288→        return (result.returncode == 0, output)
   289→    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   290→        return (False, f"Command timed out after {timeout}s: {cmd}")
   291→    except Exception as e:
   292→        return (False, f"Command failed: {e}")
   293→
   294→
   295→def get_commit_hash(worktree_path: str) -> Optional[str]:
   296→    """
   297→    Get the current commit hash in a worktree.
   298→
   299→    Args:
   300→        worktree_path: Path to the worktree
   301→
   302→    Returns:
   303→        Commit hash, or None if not in a git repo
   304→
   305→    Raises:
   306→        GitWorktreeError: If getting commit hash fails
   307→    """
   308→    try:
   309→        result = subprocess.run(
   310→            ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
   311→            cwd=str(worktree_path),
   312→            check=True,
   313→            capture_output=True,
   314→            text=True,
   315→        )
   316→        return result.stdout.strip()
   317→    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
   318→        return None
   319→
   320→
   321→def has_diff_from_main(worktree_path: str, main_branch: str = "main") -> bool:
   322→    """
   323→    Check if the worktree branch has any commits relative to main branch.
   324→
   325→    Runs 'git log main..HEAD --oneline' and returns True if output is non-empty.
   326→    This detects if the worker actually committed any changes.
   327→
   328→    Args:
   329→        worktree_path: Path to the worktree
   330→        main_branch: Main branch to compare against (default: main)
   331→
   332→    Returns:
   333→        True if there are commits ahead of main, False otherwise
   334→
   335→    Raises:
   336→        GitWorktreeError: If git command fails
   337→    """
   338→    try:
   339→        result = subprocess.run(
   340→            ["git", "log", f"{main_branch}..HEAD", "--oneline"],
   341→            cwd=str(worktree_path),
   342→            check=True,
   343→            capture_output=True,
   344→            text=True,
   345→        )
   346→        return bool(result.stdout.strip())
   347→    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
   348→        raise GitWorktreeError(f"Failed to check diff from main: {e.stderr}") from e
   349→
   350→
   351→# --- Async wrappers ---
   352→# These run blocking git operations in a thread executor to avoid
   353→# blocking the asyncio event loop. Use these from async code instead
   354→# of calling the sync versions directly.
   355→
   356→
   357→async def create_worktree_async(project_path: str, agent_name: str, base_branch: str = "main") -> str:
   358→    """Async wrapper for create_worktree. Runs in a thread executor."""
   359→    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   360→    return await loop.run_in_executor(None, create_worktree, project_path, agent_name, base_branch)
   361→
   362→
   363→async def remove_worktree_async(worktree_path: str) -> bool:
   364→    """Async wrapper for remove_worktree. Runs in a thread executor."""
   365→    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   366→    return await loop.run_in_executor(None, remove_worktree, worktree_path)
   367→
   368→
   369→async def rebase_onto_main_async(worktree_path: str, main_branch: str = "main") -> bool:
   370→    """Async wrapper for rebase_onto_main. Runs in a thread executor."""
   371→    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   372→    return await loop.run_in_executor(None, rebase_onto_main, worktree_path, main_branch)
   373→
   374→
   375→async def abort_rebase_async(worktree_path: str):
   376→    """Async wrapper for abort_rebase. Runs in a thread executor."""
   377→    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   378→    await loop.run_in_executor(None, abort_rebase, worktree_path)
   379→
   380→
   381→async def merge_to_main_async(project_path: str, branch_name: str, main_branch: str = "main"):
   382→    """Async wrapper for merge_to_main. Runs in a thread executor."""
   383→    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   384→    await loop.run_in_executor(None, merge_to_main, project_path, branch_name, main_branch)
   385→
   386→
   387→async def get_worktree_dirty_status_async(project_path: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
   388→    """Async wrapper for get_worktree_dirty_status. Runs in a thread executor."""
   389→    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   390→    return await loop.run_in_executor(None, get_worktree_dirty_status, project_path)
   391→
   392→
   393→async def run_command_in_worktree_async(worktree_path: str, cmd: str, timeout: int = 300) -> tuple:
   394→    """Async wrapper for run_command_in_worktree. Runs in a thread executor."""
   395→    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   396→    return await loop.run_in_executor(None, run_command_in_worktree, worktree_path, cmd, timeout)
   397→
   398→
   399→async def delete_branch_async(project_path: str, branch_name: str, force: bool = False):
   400→    """Async wrapper for delete_branch. Runs in a thread executor."""
   401→    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   402→    return await loop.run_in_executor(None, delete_branch, project_path, branch_name, force)
   403→
   404→
   405→async def has_diff_from_main_async(worktree_path: str, main_branch: str = "main") -> bool:
   406→    """Async wrapper for has_diff_from_main. Runs in a thread executor."""
   407→    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   408→    return await loop.run_in_executor(None, has_diff_from_main, worktree_path, main_branch)

645→    async def main_loop(self):
   646→        """Main orchestration loop."""
   647→        while self.running:
   648→            try:
   649→                # Check if OpenCode is healthy before scheduling work
   650→                if not self._opencode_healthy:
   651→                    # In degraded mode - check health with exponential backoff
   652→                    healthy = await self._check_opencode_health()
   653→
   654→                    if healthy:
   655→                        # OpenCode recovered
   656→                        degraded_duration = (datetime.now() - self._degraded_since).total_seconds() if self._degraded_since else 0
   657→                        self.db.log_system_event(
   658→                            "opencode_recovered", {"degraded_duration_seconds": degraded_duration, "backoff_delay": self._backoff_delay}
   659→                        )
   660→                        self._opencode_healthy = True
   661→                        self._degraded_since = None
   662→                        self._backoff_delay = 5  # Reset backoff

   664→                    else:

   670→                # Per-run budget cap — stop spawning if total token spend exceeds limit

   678→                        await asyncio.sleep(Config.POLL_INTERVAL)

   709→                if self._opencode_healthy:

content.content:
commit d9e1c57ddfbb7fd298b1fa598fdffd84e6ccd4eb
Author: Tommy Bui Nguyen <tommynguyen0512@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 23 17:27:27 2026 +0800

    fix dep-wiring race and merge cleanup leaks
    
    1. Atomic dep wiring: create_issue() now accepts depends_on and
       inserts deps in the same transaction as the issue. The issue is
       never visible to get_ready_queue without its blocking deps.
       cli.py delegates to db.create_issue(depends_on=...) instead of
       separate add_dependency calls. Queen prompt updated to emphasize
       --depends-on at creation time.
    
    2. Merge cleanup: _cleanup_merge_resources() now runs on all non-
       success paths (rejection, escalation, git merge failure, refinery
       errors). Previously only the happy path called _teardown_after_
       finalize, leaving orphaned worktrees, branches, and agent rows.
    
    3. Monitor exception path: monitor_agent now calls _teardown_agent
       in its except block so a crashed monitor doesn't leak the agent
       from active_agents.
    
    Entire-Checkpoint: 880c3cbd0199

content.content:
def create_issue(
        self,
        title: str,
        description: str = "",
        priority: int = 2,
        issue_type: str = "task",
        project: str = "",
        parent_id: Optional[str] = None,
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        tags: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        depends_on: Optional[list[str]] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        Create a new issue.

        Dependencies are wired in the same transaction as the INSERT so
        the issue is never visible to get_ready_queue without its deps.

        Args:
            title: Issue title
            description: Issue description
            priority: Priority (0=critical, 4=low)
            issue_type: Type (task, bug, feature, step, epic)
            project: Project/repo name
            parent_id: Parent issue ID (for epics)
            model: Model to use for this issue (overrides global WORKER_MODEL)
            tags: List of tags for the issue (validated against ALLOWED_TAGS)
            metadata: Additional metadata dict
            depends_on: List of issue IDs this issue depends on (blocks type)

        Returns:
            Generated issue ID
        """
        issue_id = generate_id("w")
        metadata_json = json.dumps(metadata) if metadata else None

        # Validate and serialize tags
        tags_json = None
        if tags:
            validated_tags = validate_tags(tags)
            tags_json = json.dumps(validated_tags)

        with self.transaction() as conn:
            conn.execute(
                """
                INSERT INTO issues (id, title, description, priority, type, project, parent_id, model, tags, metadata)
                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
                """,
                (
                    issue_id,
                    title,
                    description,
                    priority,
                    issue_type,
                    project,
                    parent_id,
                    model,
                    tags_json,
                    metadata_json,
                ),
            )

            # Wire deps in the same transaction — the issue is never visible
            # to get_ready_queue without its blocking dependencies.
            if depends_on:
                for dep_id in depends_on:
                    conn.execute(
                        "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on, type) VALUES (?, ?, 'blocks')",
                        (issue_id, dep_id),
                    )

            self.log_event(issue_id, None, "created", {"title": title}, commit=False)

        return issue_id

    def get_ready_queue(self, project: Optional[str] = None, limit: Optional[int] = None) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Query for ready work items.

        Returns issues that are:

   673→                    if run_tokens > Config.MAX_TOKENS_PER_RUN:
   674→                        if not self._budget_paused:
   675→                            logger.warning(f"Run budget exceeded ({run_tokens} tokens). Pausing new spawns.")
   676→                            self._budget_paused = True
   677→                            self.db.log_system_event("budget_paused", {"total_tokens": run_tokens})
   678→                        await asyncio.sleep(Config.POLL_INTERVAL)
   679→                        # Still check for stalled agents even when budget-paused
   680→                        if self._opencode_healthy:
   681→                            await self.check_stalled_agents()
   682→                        continue
   683→
   684→                # Normal operation - check if we can spawn more agents
   685→                if len(self.active_agents) < Config.MAX_AGENTS:
   686→                    # Get ready work
   687→                    ready = self.db.get_ready_queue(project=self.project_name, limit=1)
   688→
   689→                    if ready:
   690→                        issue = ready[0]
   691→                        if issue["id"] in self._spawning_issues:
   692→                            await asyncio.sleep(1)  # Back off briefly while spawn completes
   693→                            continue
   694→                        try:
   695→                            # Try to claim and spawn worker
   696→                            await self.spawn_worker(issue)
   697→                        except Exception as e:
   698→                            # Check if the error suggests OpenCode is unhealthy
   699→                            if self._is_opencode_error(e):
   700→                                await self._enter_degraded_mode(str(e))
   701→                    else:
   702→                        # No ready work, wait before polling again
   703→                        await asyncio.sleep(Config.POLL_INTERVAL)
   704→                else:
   705→                    # At capacity, wait
   706→                    await asyncio.sleep(Config.POLL_INTERVAL)
   707→
   708→                # Check for stalled agents (only when healthy)
   709→                if self._opencode_healthy:
   710→                    await self.check_stalled_agents()
   711→
   712→            except Exception as e:
   713→                # Check if this is an OpenCode connectivity issue
   714→                if self._is_opencode_error(e):
   715→                    await self._enter_degraded_mode(str(e))
   716→                else:
   717→                    logger.error(f"Error in main loop: {e}")
   718→                    await asyncio.sleep(Config.POLL_INTERVAL)
   719→

720→    async def spawn_worker(self, issue: Dict[str, str]):
   721→        """
   722→        Spawn a worker to handle an issue.
   723→        Always creates a new agent.
   724→
   725→        Args:
   726→            issue: Issue dict from database
   727→        """
   728→        issue_id = issue["id"]
   729→        self._spawning_issues.add(issue_id)
   730→        try:
   731→            await self._spawn_worker_inner(issue)
   732→        finally:
   733→            self._spawning_issues.discard(issue_id)
   734→
   735→    async def _spawn_worker_inner(self, issue: Dict[str, str]):
   736→        """Inner spawn logic, wrapped by spawn_worker's TOCTOU guard."""
   737→        issue_id = issue["id"]
   738→        agent_name = generate_id("worker")
   739→        model = issue.get("model") or Config.WORKER_MODEL or Config.DEFAULT_MODEL
   740→
   741→        # Create agent identity in database
   742→        agent_id = self.db.create_agent(
   743→            name=agent_name,
   744→            model=model,
   745→            metadata={"issue_id": issue_id},
   746→            project=self.project_name,
   747→        )
   748→
   749→        # Create git worktree (in executor to avoid blocking event loop)
   750→        try:
   751→            worktree_path = await create_worktree_async(str(self.project_path), agent_name)
   752→        except Exception as e:
   753→            self.db.log_event(
   754→                issue_id,
   755→                agent_id,
   756→                "worktree_error",
   757→                {"error": _exc_detail(e)},
   758→            )
   759→            self._delete_agent_row(agent_id)
   760→            return
   761→
   762→        # Atomic claim
   763→        claimed = self.db.claim_issue(issue_id, agent_id)
   764→        if not claimed:
   765→            # Someone else claimed it first, clean up worktree and delete agent
   766→            await remove_worktree_async(worktree_path)
   767→            self._delete_agent_row(agent_id)
   768→            return
   769→
   770→        # Create OpenCode session
   771→        session_id = None  # Track for cleanup on failure
   772→        try:
   773→            session = await self.opencode.create_session(
   774→                directory=worktree_path,
   775→                title=f"{agent_name}: {issue['title']}",
   776→                permissions=WORKER_PERMISSIONS,
   777→            )
   778→            session_id = session["id"]
   779→
   780→            # Update agent with session info
   781→            self.db.conn.execute(
   782→                """
   783→                UPDATE agents
   784→                SET session_id = ?,
   785→                    worktree = ?,
   786→                    lease_expires_at = datetime('now', '+{} seconds'),
   787→                    last_progress_at = datetime('now')
   788→                WHERE id = ?
   789→                """.format(Config.LEASE_DURATION),
   790→                (session_id, worktree_path, agent_id),
   791→            )
   792→            self.db.conn.commit()
   793→
   794→            # Create agent identity
   795→            agent = AgentIdentity(
   796→                agent_id=agent_id,
   797→                name=agent_name,
   798→                issue_id=issue_id,
   799→                worktree=worktree_path,
   800→                session_id=session_id,
   801→            )
   802→            self._register_active_agent(agent)
   803→
   804→            await self._dispatch_worker_to_issue(
   805→                agent=agent,
   806→                issue=issue,
   807→                model=model,
   808→                started_event_type="worker_started",
   809→                started_event_detail={
   810→                    "session_id": session_id,
   811→                    "worktree": worktree_path,
   812→                    "routing_method": "new_agent",
   813→                    "prompt_version": get_prompt_version("worker"),
   814→                    "model": model,
   815→                },
   816→            )
   817→
   818→        except Exception as e:
   819→            self.db.log_event(
   820→                issue_id,
   821→                agent_id,
   822→                "spawn_error",
   823→                {"error": _exc_detail(e)},
   824→            )
   825→            # Clean up the OpenCode session if it was created (best-effort —
   826→            # don't let cleanup failure prevent DB/worktree cleanup below)
   827→            if session_id:
   828→                await self._best_effort_cleanup(
   829→                    "spawn_session_cleanup",
   830→                    self.opencode.cleanup_session(session_id, directory=worktree_path),
   831→                )
   832→            # Clean up in-memory tracking (if agent was registered)
   833→            if agent_id in self.active_agents:
   834→                self._unregister_agent(agent_id)
   835→            # Mark agent as failed in DB
   836→            self._mark_agent_failed(agent_id)
   837→            # Clean up worktree and mark issue failed
   838→            await remove_worktree_async(worktree_path)
   839→            self.db.update_issue_status(issue_id, "failed")
   840→
   841→    def _gather_notes_for_worker(self, issue_id: str) -> Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
   842→        """Gather relevant notes to inject into a worker's prompt.

   844→        Combines epic-specific notes (if the issue is a step) with
   845→        recent project-wide notes, deduplicating by note ID.
   846→
   847→        Returns None if no notes are found (so build_worker_prompt skips the section).
   848→        """
